chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يَحْتَجِمُ أَوْ يَقُصُّ ظُفُراً أَوْ شَعْراً أَوْ شَيْئاً مِنْهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 95 – The one in Ihraam gets cupping done, or he clips nails, or hair, or something from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/322)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/322)
text: إِنْ نَتَفَ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنْ شَعْرِ لِحْيَتِهِ وَ غَيْرِهَا شَيْئاً فَعَلَيْهِ أَنْ يُطْعِمَ مِسْكِيناً فِي يَدِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘If the one in Ihraam were to pluck from the hair of his beard, or anything from other than it, so upon him is that he feeds a poor one in his hand’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one in the state of Ihram pulls out a hair from his beard or from other parts of his body, he must feed one destitute person in his hand (with one meal).”’
